Description

Using either NVCC or NVRTC backends, my kernels cannot include system wide headers on windows 11 with CUDA 12.4 / MSVC 143 (x64).
Altough it build fine on a normal Visual Studio CUDA project.



### To Reproduce

Example to demonstrate that system-wide headers are not included

```py
import cupy as cp

CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK = 512

kernel = cp.RawKernel(r"""
#include <cfloat>

#define F_MAX FLT_MAX

extern "C" __global__ void test(int n, double *a, double *b, float *c)
{
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i < n)
    {
        double r = a[i] + b[i];
        if (r > F_MAX)
            r = F_MAX;
        c[i] = r;
    }
}
""","test")

n = 1024
res = cp.zeros((n,), dtype=cp.float32)
a = cp.full((n,), 1.0, dtype=cp.float64)
b = cp.full((n,), 2.0, dtype=cp.float64)
test_kernel(
    ((n + CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK - 1) // CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK,),
    (CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK,),
    (n, a, b, res),
)

assert cp.all(res == 3.0)
```

Description

Using either NVCC or NVRTC backends, my kernels cannot include system wide headers on windows 11 with CUDA 12.4 / MSVC 143 (x64).
Altough it build fine on a normal Visual Studio CUDA project.


### To Reproduce

Example to demonstrate that system-wide headers are not included

```py
import cupy as cp

CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK = 512

kernel = cp.RawKernel(r"""
#include <cfloat>

#define F_MAX FLT_MAX

extern "C" __global__ void test(int n, double *a, double *b, float *c)
{
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i < n)
    {
        double r = a[i] + b[i];
        if (r > F_MAX)
            r = F_MAX;
        c[i] = r;
    }
}
""","test")

n = 1024
res = cp.zeros((n,), dtype=cp.float32)
a = cp.full((n,), 1.0, dtype=cp.float64)
b = cp.full((n,), 2.0, dtype=cp.float64)
kernel(
    ((n + CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK - 1) // CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK,),
    (CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK,),
    (n, a, b, res),
)

assert cp.all(res == 3.0)
```

error: identifier "FLT_MAX" is undefined

Description

Using either NVCC or NVRTC backends, my kernels cannot include system wide headers on windows 11 with CUDA 12.4 / MSVC 143 (x64).
Altough it build fine on a normal Visual Studio CUDA project.


### To Reproduce

Example to demonstrate that system-wide headers are not included

```py
import cupy as cp

CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK = 512

kernel = cp.RawKernel(r"""
#include <cfloat>

#define F_MAX FLT_MAX

extern "C" __global__ void test(int n, double *a, double *b, float *c)
{
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i < n)
    {
        double r = a[i] + b[i];
        if (r > F_MAX)
            r = F_MAX;
        c[i] = r;
    }
}
""","test")

n = 1024
res = cp.zeros((n,), dtype=cp.float32)
a = cp.full((n,), 1.0, dtype=cp.float64)
b = cp.full((n,), 2.0, dtype=cp.float64)
kernel(
    ((n + CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK - 1) // CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK,),
    (CUDA_MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK,),
    (n, a, b, res),
)

assert cp.all(res == 3.0)
```

Error output:
error: identifier "FLT_MAX" is undefined
